subUnits.title: 2.1 Kingdom Monera and Kingdom Protista
notes: Classification is the arrangement of organisms into groups based on shared characteristics. This makes the study of diverse organisms manageable and meaningful.
Organisms are classified into kingdoms based on:
• Cell type: Prokaryotic (no nucleus) vs Eukaryotic (with nucleus)
• Mode of nutrition: Autotrophic vs Heterotrophic
• Eukaryotes: Kingdoms Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia
• Prokaryotic cells (no membrane-bound nucleus or organelles)
• Diverse metabolic strategies: photosynthetic, chemosynthetic, heterotrophic
• Cell walls present (made of peptidoglycan in bacteria)
• Nitrogen-fixing bacteria (Rhizobium in legume root nodules)
• Lactic acid bacteria (used in ogi production)
• Soil bacteria for nitrogen fixation in Nigerian farms
• Cyanobacteria in rice fields contribute to soil fertility
• Lactic acid bacteria in traditional food fermentation (ogi, kunun-zaki)
• Food production: fermentation (yogurt, cheese, local foods)
• Digestion: gut bacteria aid in food breakdown
• Pathogens: cause diseases in humans, animals, plants
• Mostly unicellular, some colonial or simple multicellular
• Reproduction: asexual (binary fission) and sexual (in some)
3. Slime molds and water molds (Fungus-like protists):
• Plasmodium falciparum causes malaria - major health challenge
• Protozoa in soil ecosystems contribute to nutrient cycling
• Algae in fish ponds serve as food for fish
• Some algae cause harmful algal blooms in water bodies
• Euglena found in stagnant water in Nigerian communities
• Teacher grows culture of bacteria and blue-green algae
• Students observe the cultures, record observations, and make inferences
• Discussion: What characteristics do these organisms share?
• Prepare wet mount slides of pond water
• Observe under microscope for protists (Amoeba, Paramecium, Spirogyra)
• 'All bacteria are harmful': Many bacteria are beneficial or essential
• 'Protists are just simple organisms': They exhibit complex behaviors and life cycles
• 'All protists are microscopic': Some algae are large (e.g., kelp)
• 'Prokaryotes are primitive': They are highly adapted and successful
• Kingdom Monera includes prokaryotic organisms (bacteria and cyanobacteria)
• Kingdom Protista includes diverse unicellular eukaryotes
• Both kingdoms play crucial roles in ecosystems and human life
• Understanding these kingdoms is foundational for studying biology
• In Nigeria, knowledge of these organisms helps address health, agriculture, and environmental challenges

subUnits.youtubeQuery: Kingdom Monera Protista biology classification SSS1 site:youtube.com

quiz.explanation: Monera includes prokaryotes (bacteria and cyanobacteria) that lack a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

quiz.q: An example of a protist that causes disease in Nigeria is:
quiz.explanation: Plasmodium falciparum is a protozoan parasite that causes malaria, a major health challenge in Nigeria.

quiz.q: The main difference between prokaryotes and eukaryotes is:
opts: Presence of membrane-bound nucleus in eukaryotes
quiz.explanation: Eukaryotes have a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, while prokaryotes do not.

quiz.q: Cyanobacteria are important in Nigerian agriculture because they:
opts: Fix nitrogen and enrich soil fertility
quiz.explanation: Cyanobacteria in rice paddies and soils fix atmospheric nitrogen, contributing to soil fertility.

quiz.q: A characteristic of Kingdom Protista is:
quiz.explanation: Protists are mostly unicellular eukaryotes with diverse modes of nutrition (autotrophic, heterotrophic, or mixotrophic).

quiz.q: Nitrogen-fixing bacteria in legume root nodules belong to Kingdom:
quiz.explanation: Nitrogen-fixing bacteria like Rhizobium are prokaryotes belonging to Kingdom Monera.

quiz.q: Spirogyra is classified under Kingdom Protista because it:
opts: Is a unicellular or filamentous eukaryote
quiz.explanation: Spirogyra is a filamentous green alga with eukaryotic cells, placing it in Kingdom Protista.

quiz.q: A beneficial role of bacteria in Nigerian food production is:
opts: Lactic acid fermentation in ogi and kunun-zaki
quiz.explanation: Lactic acid bacteria are used in traditional Nigerian food fermentation processes like ogi and kunun-zaki production.

quiz.q: The five kingdoms are classified into prokaryotes and eukaryotes as:
opts: Prokaryotes: Monera; Eukaryotes: Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia
quiz.explanation: Kingdom Monera contains prokaryotes, while Kingdoms Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia contain eukaryotes.

quiz.q: Understanding Kingdom Monera and Protista is important for Nigerian health because it:
opts: Helps understand and control diseases like malaria and bacterial infections
quiz.explanation: Knowledge of these kingdoms helps understand disease-causing organisms like Plasmodium (malaria) and pathogenic bacteria.
